description:
You are a capacity-planning analyst who has just received a raw CSV export of historical
  machine–resource metrics.  
  The file:

    /home/user/data/usage.csv

  already exists and has the following exact contents (including the header line):

    host,timestamp,cpu,memory
    host1,2023-01-01T00:00:00Z,57,40
    host1,2023-01-01T01:00:00Z,63,43
    host1,2023-01-01T02:00:00Z,50,38
    host2,2023-01-01T00:00:00Z,80,70
    host2,2023-01-01T01:00:00Z,75,68
    host2,2023-01-01T02:00:00Z,82,71
    host3,2023-01-01T00:00:00Z,20,30
    host3,2023-01-01T01:00:00Z,25,28
    host3,2023-01-01T02:00:00Z,22,29

  Your task:

  1. In **one single terminal command** (pipelines are fine, but no multi-line scripts),
     calculate the average CPU utilisation (the “cpu” column) for each distinct host.

  2. Write the result to the file

       /home/user/output/summary.txt

     so that it is **exactly** three lines long, sorted in descending order of the
     average CPU value, and formatted like this:

       hostname␠average_cpu

     where
     • “hostname” is the string from the “host” column  
     • “average_cpu” is the arithmetic mean of that host’s “cpu” values, rounded to
       exactly two decimal places.  
     • Use a single space character (U+0020) to separate the two fields, and terminate
       each line with a UNIX newline (LF, \n). No header, no extra blank lines.

  Example of the required final file format (actual values must match the calculation
  from the provided CSV):

    hostX 99.99
    hostY 88.88
    hostZ 77.77

  Notes/constraints:
  • You may assume /home/user/output exists or create it if necessary.  
  • Do **not** edit /home/user/data/usage.csv.  
  • Accomplish the transformation with a single shell command (you may combine
    utilities with pipes).  
  • Do not include explanatory text in the output file—only the three data lines
    in the precise format described above.  
  • Overwrite /home/user/output/summary.txt if it already exists.  

  When finished, you should be able to display the file and see exactly three lines
  showing each host followed by its two-decimal-place average CPU, ordered from
  highest to lowest.
truth:
The correct /home/user/output/summary.txt file must exist and contain exactly:

    host2 79.00
    host1 56.67
    host3 22.33

  (Each line ends with a single LF newline, no trailing blank line after the last
   line.)

  File paths that exist before the task starts:
    /home/user/data/usage.csv   – with the CSV content specified in the public block.
    /home/user/output           – empty directory; writeable by the unprivileged user.

  Permissions: All files/directories are owned by user:user, mode 0755 for directories,
  0644 for files.

  The automated test will:
    • Verify that /home/user/output/summary.txt exists.  
    • Compare its contents byte-for-byte with the block above.  
    • Ensure that no additional files were modified under /home/user/data.  
    • Confirm that the agent accomplished the task without executing scripts outside
      a single command context.
name: task_000000_f52f68cc
